news.summary: The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company reported first quarter 2025 results with net sales of $4.3 billion and a net income of $115 million. The first quarter included a gain of $260 million from the sale of the Off-the-Road tire business and rationalization charges of $81 million. Goodyear's segment operating income was $195 million, down $52 million from the previous year. The company's transformation plan, Goodyear Forward, aims to achieve $1.5 billion in annual benefits by the end of 2025. Goodyear also completed the sale of the Dunlop brand for $735 million. The Americas segment had net sales of $2.5 billion, the EMEA segment reported net sales of $1.3 billion, and the Asia Pacific segment had net sales of $474 million. Each segment experienced changes in tire unit volumes and operating incomes compared to the prior year. Goodyear experienced decreases in net sales due to various factors including changes in foreign exchange rates and declines in tire volumes. The company plans to use the proceeds from recent sales to reduce leverage and fund initiatives. Further details can be found on Goodyear's investor relations website.
news.headline: Goodyear reports Q1 2025 results, completes Dunlop brand sale. Net sales $4.3B, $115M net income. Segment operating income $195M. Target segment operating margin 10%. Forward benefits $200M. Dunlop brand sale for $735M. Initiatives focus on margin expansion and reducing leverage. Segment operating income down $52M driven by higher raw materials. Cash flow negative due to working capital. Business segments report mixed results. EMEA reports segment operating loss. Asia Pacific reports lower net sales. Forward-looking plan expected to deliver $1.5B in benefits by 2025. Sales proceeds to reduce leverage and fund plan. Non-GAAP financial measures detailed. Visit investor relations website for more info. Company employs 68,000 people, operates in 53 facilities across 20 countries. Meeting planned for investor updates. Full story on Benzinga.

news.summary: Kiwetinohk Energy Corp. reported its first quarter 2025 results with record quarterly production of 32,611 boe/d, generating $29.5 million in positive free funds flow. The company also gained $21 million from the sale of their Opal gas-fired power project. They engaged financial firms to review potential value-enhancing opportunities, including selling assets or merging with another entity. Financially, commodity sales from production totaled $168,392, with cash flow from operating activities at $110,317. The company's net income amounted to $54,919, with a net debt of $234,839 as of March 31, 2025. Operating expenses decreased from $7.03/boe in 2024 to $5.20/boe in 2025, contributing to a stronger balance sheet. The company's strategy included profitable growth in the upstream business while exploring opportunities to monetize power development projects.
news.headline: Kiwetinohk Energy Corp. reports strong Q1 2025 results - production of 32,611 boe/d, $29.5M in free cash flow, and $168.4M in commodity sales. Strategic review initiated for value enhancement.

news.summary: In Salt Lake City, Cyrq Energy, LLC reveals a significant deal to sell its Blue Mountain geothermal power facility in Nevada for $88 million to an Ormat Technologies, Inc. subsidiary, in accordance with its five-year strategic plan. A prominent player in geothermal power development since 2007, Cyrq is headquartered in Salt Lake City. The company currently possesses multiple power generating facilities, such as the Soda Lake and Patua geothermal power plants.
news.headline: Cyrq Energy sells Blue Mountain facility in Nevada for $88 million as part of 5-year strategic plan. Leader in geothermal power since 2007.

news.summary: A recent study found that 25% of adults aged 18-35 have experienced mental health issues in the past year. The research surveyed 10,000 individuals and highlighted the impact of social media on mental well-being, with 70% reporting feelings of anxiety or depression linked to their online activities. The study also revealed that only 40% of those affected sought professional help, indicating a need for increased awareness and access to mental health resources among young adults. The findings underscore the growing concern over the mental health challenges facing this demographic and call for proactive measures to address these issues effectively.
news.headline: Watts Water Technologies announced Q1 2025 results.

news.summary: In St. Louis, the Missouri Public Service Commission (MoPSC) has recently given the green light to new water and wastewater rates for Missouri American Water. The company had submitted a rate request on July 1, 2024, citing $1.1 billion worth of investments in upgrading plant and distribution systems as the key factor behind the proposal. This move underscores their dedication to ensuring the provision of safe, clean, reliable, and cost-effective water and wastewater services to consumers.
news.headline: Missouri American Water gets new rates approved by MoPSC after $1.1 billion in infrastructure investments.

news.summary: Houston-based specialty chemical company, Orion S.A. (NYSE: OEC), released their financial performance for the quarter ending March 31, 2025. Key numbers from the report include: Net sales dropped by $25.2 million year over year to $477.7 million. Net income saw a decline of $17.6 million year over year, resting at $9.1 million. Diluted earnings per share (EPS) were at $0.16, down by $0.29 compared to the previous year. Adjusted EBITDA stood at $66.2 million, marking a 22% decrease from the previous year. Adjusted Diluted EPS settled at $0.22, down $0.30 year over year. These results indicate a challenging quarter for Orion S.A.
news.headline: Orion S.A. reports Q1 2025 financial results: net sales $477.7M, net income $9.1M, diluted EPS $0.16, adjusted EBITDA $66.2M.

news.summary: In a recent announcement made on May 7, 2025, Ramp, a rapidly growing corporate card and financial operations platform in the United States, has revealed plans to broaden its issuing collaboration with Stripe, a leading programmable financial services firm. This strategic move aims to introduce the market's inaugural stablecoin-supported corporate cards. This initiative is part of Ramp's commitment to innovation and growth, solidifying its position in the financial sector. The specific figures related to this expansion were not provided in the announcement.
news.headline: Ramp and Stripe partner to launch stablecoin-backed corporate cards, deepening their global commerce impact.

news.summary: In Davidson, North Carolina, Curtiss-Wright has announced robust first quarter results for 2025, surpassing expectations. news.summary: EL DORADO, Ark.--Murphy USA Inc. reported their financial results for the three months ending on March 31, 2025. In Q1 2025, the company recorded a net income of $53.2 million, equating to $2.63 per diluted share, a decrease from $66.0 million and $3.12 per diluted share in Q1 2024. The total fuel contribution for Q1 2025 stood at 25.4 cpg, up from 24.8 cpg in the same period in 2024. Additionally, total retail gallons decreased compared to the previous year.
news.headline: Murphy USA reports Q1 2025 results: Net income $53.2 million, fuel contribution 25.4 cpg, retail gallons decreased.

This marks a substantial milestone for IIP in its mission to lead in the cannabis real estate industry.

news.summary: Houston-based Civeo Corporation (NYSE:CVEO) has successfully finalized the acquisition of four villages featuring 1,340 rooms situated in Australia's Bowen Basin, along with the related customer contracts. This strategic transaction required a total cash payout of A$105 million, which equates to around US$67 million. The company financed this purchase through a combination of available cash reserves and loans from their current revolving credit facility. This move marks a significant step for Civeo, as indicated by the company's satisfaction in concluding this crucial acquisition ahead of schedule.
news.headline: Civeo acquires 4 villages with 1,340 rooms in Australian Bowen Basin for $105 million. Acquisition funded by cash and borrowings.

Additionally, Excelerate reported an Adjusted EBITDA of $100.4 million for the first quarter. The company revised its Full Year 2025 Adjusted EBITDA guidance, now anticipating it to be within the range of $345 million to $365 million.
news.headline: Excelerate Energy reports Q1 2025 Net Income of $52.1M, Adjusted Net Income of $55.6M, and Adjusted EBITDA of $100.4M. FY guidance raised to $345-365M.

news.summary: In Norton Shores, Michigan, EarthTronics has launched its Wattage Selectable Washdown LED Series with EarthConnect Bluetooth Mesh Control for applications like food processing and clean rooms. This series, ideal for areas requiring washdown and product safety, is NEMA 4X rated and ETL Sanitation listed, meeting NSF STD.2 standards for uses in commercial kitchens and freezers. This innovation by EarthTronics utilizes energy-saving lighting and offers significant economic and environmental benefits.
news.headline: EarthTronics introduces Washdown LED Highbay Series with EarthConnect Controls for food processing. NEMA 4X rated. ETL Sanitation listed.

news.summary: In recent developments, it has been reported that the unemployment rate has reached a new high of 7.9%, affecting approximately 12.6 million individuals. This surge in joblessness is a concerning trend, especially in the current economic climate. Additionally, there has been a 15% decrease in GDP growth, with the economy showing signs of strain. These numbers highlight the challenges being faced by many in the workforce, with experts predicting a slow recovery in the coming months. The government is being urged to take action to address this growing crisis and provide support to those impacted.
news.headline: Nine Energy Service reports Q1 2025 earnings.

news.summary: Dallas-based Atmos Energy Corporation (NYSE: ATO) has announced a quarterly dividend of 87.0 cents per share for its common stock, resulting in an annual dividend of $3.48. The payment is scheduled for June 9, 2025, with a record date of May 27, 2025. This marks the company's 166th consecutive quarterly dividend. Atmos Energy Corporation, a natural gas distributor, is headquartered in Dallas and is listed as an S&P 500 company. Their primary focus is on the safe delivery of natural gas.
news.headline: Atmos Energy (ATO) declares quarterly dividend of 87.0 cents per share, annual dividend of $3.48. 166th consecutive dividend.

news.summary: In San Dimas, California, American States Water Company (NYSE: AWR) announced earnings of $0.70 per share for the quarter ending March 31, 2025, up from $0.62 per share in the same quarter of 2024. The company's consolidated results in the first quarter showed a positive growth trend.

news.summary: Dallas-based Atmos Energy Corporation (NYSE: ATO) released their second fiscal quarter results for the period ending March 31, 2025. The company reported earnings per diluted share at $5.26, resulting in a net income of $837.4 million. They invested heavily in capital expenditures, with a total of $1,730.9 million, prioritizing safety and reliability with 85% of the expenditure dedicated to these areas. The details can be found on their Form 10-Q and earnings slides on their website, www.atmosenergy.com.
news.headline: Atmos Energy Corp reports $5.26 EPS with $837.4 million net income in Q2 2025, raises guidance. Major capex of $1.73 billion.

news.summary: Mercado Libre, the leading e-commerce and fintech platform in Latin America, began the year with strong momentum in the first quarter of 2025 with net revenue and financial income increasing by 37% YoY to $5.9 billion. The income from operations rose by 45% YoY to $763 million, and net income reached $494 million, a 44% YoY increase. Unique buyers in commerce reached almost 67 million, sustaining the highest level of growth since early 2021. Fintech monthly active users crossed 64 million, growing 31% YoY. Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) rose by 17% YoY to $13.3 billion with 40% FX Neutral growth. In particular, Argentina saw spectacular performance, with FX-neutral GMV surging 126% YoY. Items sold increased 28% in Q1'25 to reach 492 million units. Brand preference reached all-time highs in key markets like Brazil, Mexico, Argentina, and Chile. The supermarket category saw the highest growth of 65% YoY in Q1. Mercado Libre continues to strengthen its commerce business and improve its value proposition.
news.headline: Mercado Libre reports strong Q1 2025 results: $5.9B revenue, $494M net income, 67M unique buyers, 64M fintech users.

news.summary: Star Group, L.P. announced its fiscal 2025 second-quarter financial results with total revenue increasing by 11.6% to $743.0 million compared to the prior year, driven by a 22.9% rise in home heating oil and propane sales totaling 143.9 million gallons. Net income rose by $17.5 million to $85.9 million, with Adjusted EBITDA reaching $128.2 million, up from $96.3 million in fiscal 2024. For the first six months of fiscal 2025, total revenue increased by 3.1% to $1.2 billion, with a 14.7% increase in home heating oil and propane sales to 226.3 million gallons. Net income rose to $118.8 million, primarily due to higher Adjusted EBITDA of $180.0 million. Adjusted EBITDA increased by $34.7 million attributed to higher margins and volumes, despite a $10.6 million reduction from the company's weather hedge. The company reported an expense of $3.1 million under weather hedge contracts due to colder temperatures, unlike the previous year, which recorded a credit of $7.5 million. Star Group's management plans to host a webcast and conference call to discuss these results, aiming to enhance operational efficiency and customer service. The complete financial statement is available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Star Group, L.P. reports an 11.6% increase in total revenue to $743.0 million in Q2, with a 22.9% rise in home heating oil and propane sales. Q2 net income rose to $85.9 million, with a $128.2 million Adjusted EBITDA. Year-to-date revenue increased by 3.1% to $1.2 billion.

news.summary: LivePerson, Inc., a prominent provider of trusted services, reported a total revenue of $64.7 million, exceeding the midpoint of their guidance range. Additionally, their adjusted EBITDA surpassed the high end of their guidance range. This announcement was made in New York on May 7, 2025, by the company listed on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol LPSN.
news.headline: LivePerson reports Q1 2025 results: Revenue $64.7M, exceeding guidance, EBITDA surpasses expectations.

news.summary: GXO Logistics, Inc. reported a strong first quarter in 2025, with revenue increasing by 21% year over year to $3 billion and organic revenue growth of 3%. They signed $228 million in new business wins and grew their sales pipeline to $2.5 billion. They also finalized a major deal with England's National Health Service Supply Chain worth $2.5 billion. The company repurchased 2.8 million shares and reaffirmed their full-year 2025 organic revenue growth and adjusted EBITDA guidance. Net loss for Q1 2025 was $95 million, compared to a net loss of $36 million in Q1 2024. Adjusted EBITDA was $163 million. Cash and cash equivalents were $288 million, debt outstanding was $2.7 billion, and net debt was $2.4 billion. GXO also provided guidance for 2025, including organic revenue growth of 3% to 6% and adjusted EBITDA of $840 million to $860 million. They will hold a conference call on May 8, 2025, to discuss these results.
news.headline: GXO reported strong Q1 2025 results: revenue grew 21% YoY to $3B, organic growth 3%, sales pipeline hit $2.5B, repurchased 2.8M shares, net loss $95M, adj. EBITDA $163M, cash flow $29M. Reaffirmed 2025 guidance with organic revenue growth 3%-6%, adj. EBITDA $840M-$860M, adj. Diluted EPS $2.40-$2.60. For more info, visit GXO website.

news.summary: Pizza Pizza Royalty Corp. (TSX: PZA) released financial results for the first quarter ending March 31, 2025. The company saw a 1.2% increase in same store sales, with Royalty Pool sales up 1.6%. The Quarter's Royalty Pool System Sales totaled $151.3 million, with Pizza Pizza restaurants contributing $129.8 million and Pizza 73 restaurants $21.5 million. Adjusted earnings per share remained unchanged at $0.230, and dividends paid out were $5.7 million. The company's working capital reserve decreased by $0.9 million to $5.2 million. There was a 0.9% decrease in fully-diluted basic EPS to $0.233, while adjusted EPS was consistent at $0.230. The Royalty Pool increased by 20 net restaurants to 797 in 2025. Operating 697 Pizza Pizza and 100 Pizza 73 restaurants, the company aims for a 2-3% growth in traditional restaurant network by 2025. The credit facility was extended until April 24, 2028, with an interest rate of 3.51%. System Sales for the Quarter were not directly comparable to 2024, due to the different number of restaurants in the Royalty Pool. These results should be read along with the detailed unaudited financial statements.
news.headline: Pizza Pizza Royalty Corp. reported Q1 2025 results with an increase in Royalty Pool sales by 1.6%, reaching $151.3 million. EPS was unchanged, SSSG increased by 1.2%, with Pizza 73 performing better at 4.6%. Dividends totaled $5.7 million for the same quarter. Net increase of 20 restaurants in the Royalty Pool, targeting 797 locations, including 697 Pizza Pizza and 100 Pizza 73 restaurants. Credit facility extended until April 24, 2028, with a variable interest rate starting at 3.51%. EPS decreased by 0.9% while adjusted EPS remained at $0.230.

news.summary: The global Amusement Park Market was valued at approximately USD 39.62 Billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 41.32 Billion in 2025 and is expected to grow to around USD 65.29 Billion by 2034, showing a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of about 5.21% during the forecast period 2025 to 2034. Companies like Disney, Cedar Fair, and Comcast are investing heavily in enhancing guest experiences, technology adoption, and sustainability. Technological upgrades, seasonal festivals, and events have been driving factors for market growth. High-end technology applications like AI, mobile apps, and augmented reality are revolutionizing operations within parks. Public-private collaborations in regions such as the US and Europe are accelerating smart, sustainable theme park planning. Key segments analyzed include Type, Audience, Ticket Revenue, Group Category, and Region. The research report provides detailed insights, competitive analysis, growth factors, and recent trends in the market. The report covers the market size in 2025, projected market size in 2034, with a CAGR growth rate of 5.21% from the base year 2024, and the forecast period from 2025 to 2034. Major regions covered include North America, Europe, Asia Pacific, Middle East & Africa, and South & Central America.
news.headline: Global Amusement Park Market to reach USD 65.29 billion by 2034 with 5.21% CAGR. Technological upgrades and seasonal events drive growth.

news.summary: Tel Aviv, Israel - On May 7, 2025, PainReform Ltd. (Nasdaq: PRFX) disclosed the initiation of a strategic pilot program in partnership with Econergy Renewable Energy Ltd., a prominent European independent power producer. The collaboration aims to implement PainReform’s innovative DeepSolar™ platform, an AI-driven optimization tool, to improve operational efficiency and energy output at utility-scale solar plants. The pilot initiative will take place at Econergy’s 92-megawatt (MW) photovoltaic facility located in Parau, Romania, which stands as one of the largest operational solar ventures in the country.
news.headline: PainReform partners with Econergy in AI pilot for Romania's 92 MW solar plant to boost efficiency.

news.summary: French financial markets authority regulations under Article 223-16 have been updated, impacting market operations. The revised regulations set new guidelines for market participants, promoting transparency and accountability. The changes aim to enhance investor protection and market integrity. It is crucial for all entities subject to these regulations to comply with the updated requirements to avoid penalties.Failure to adhere to the new provisions may result in significant fines or sanctions. Market players are advised to review the modifications carefully and adjust their practices accordingly to ensure full compliance with the regulatory framework.
news.headline: French financial markets authority requires monthly reporting on share capital and voting rights per Article 223-16.

news.summary: On May 7, 2025, Mercado Libre (NASDAQ: MELI), a prominent e-commerce and fintech platform in Latin America based in Montevideo, Uruguay, began the year on a high note by announcing robust performance results for the first quarter of 2025. The company experienced a remarkable 37% year-over-year increase in net revenue and financial income, reaching $5.9 billion. Moreover, income from operations surged by 45% YoY to $763 million. The net income for the quarter soared to $494 million, marking a substantial 44% YoY growth. These positive outcomes were attributed to ongoing strategic investments and enhancements to the platform's value proposition.
news.headline: In Q1 2025, Mercado Libre reports $5.9 billion revenue and $494 million net income, marking solid growth.

news.summary: In the first quarter of 2025, the net income of the company was $50.9 million, with earnings per share (EPS) at $2.21. This marks an increase from the first quarter of 2024, where the net income was $46.2 million, and EPS stood at $2.07. Adjusted net income and Adjusted EPS for the first quarter of 2025 exclude transaction and transition-related costs, showing a positive trend in financial performance.
news.headline: Chesapeake Utilities Corp. announced Q1 2025 results: Net income $50.9M, EPS $2.21; up from Q1 2024's $46.2M and $2.07.
